name: Sierra de Gúdar Cycling Loop
name_original: Puertos de Sierra de Gúdar - vuelta desde Alcalá de la Selva
description: This difficult 51.2-mile (82.4 km) road cycling route in Sierra de Gúdar offers challenging climbs and scenic mountain views.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Alcalá De La Selva are characterized by the demanding and panoramic terrain of the Sierra de Gúdar mountains. The region features significant elevation changes, with routes often starting at altitudes over 1,400 meters and including challenging climbs such as the Port of Gudar. Extensive forests of pine and holm oaks provide scenic descents and quiet minor roads, offering varied landscapes for cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Alcalá De La Selva The most popular road cycling route is Ermita de San Benón – Puerto de Valdelinares loop from Alcalá de la Selva, a 95.5 miles (153.7 km) trail that takes 7 hours 57 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is To Mora de Rubielos - return Gúdar & Javalambre, a moderate 27.3 miles (44.0 km) path. This route offers a balanced ride through varied landscapes, including sections of the Sierra de Gúdar. Local road cyclists also love the Alcalá de la Selva – Alcalá de la Selva Castle loop from Alcalá de la Selva, a 36.7 miles (59.0 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and past the historic Alcalá Castle, often completed in about 3 hours 1 minute. Road cycling around Alcalá De La Selva is defined by high mountain passes, extensive pine forests, and panoramic views across the Sierra de Gúdar. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels, with significant elevation challenges. The routes in Alcalá De La Selva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 18 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcalá De La Selva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Alcalá De La Selva?
A: Alcalá De La Selva offers a selection of 14 road cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 6 moderate and 8 difficult options, all set within the stunning Sierra de Gúdar mountains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Alcalá De La Selva?
A: You can expect demanding and panoramic terrain with significant elevation changes. Routes often start at over 1,400 meters, featuring challenging climbs like the Port of Gudar and scenic descents through extensive forests of pine and holm oaks. The region is known for its quiet minor roads, offering an immersive experience in the Sierra de Gúdar.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Alcalá De La Selva?
A: Due to its high altitude, Alcalá De La Selva offers cooler temperatures, making it a comfortable riding environment, especially during the summer months when other regions might be too hot. Spring and autumn also provide pleasant conditions with vibrant natural scenery.

Q: Are there options for less experienced road cyclists in the region?
A: While many routes are challenging, there are moderate options available. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961508235/to-mora-de-rubielos-return-gudar-javalambre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;To Mora de Rubielos - return Gúdar & Javalambre&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 44.0 km path that offers a balanced ride through varied landscapes without extreme difficulty.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in Alcalá De La Selva?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, the region offers routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20137780" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Benón – Puerto de Valdelinares loop from Alcalá de la Selva&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 153.7 km and includes significant elevation gain, making it a challenging full-day adventure.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Alcalá De La Selva?
A: Absolutely. Many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40311530" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de la Selva – Alcalá de la Selva Castle loop from Alcalá de la Selva&lt;/a&gt;, a 59.0 km trail that takes you through mountainous terrain and past the historic Alcalá Castle.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes offer stunning natural beauty. You can encounter impressive peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5805644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Summit&lt;/a&gt;, extensive pine and holm oak forests, and various springs and water features such as the Cascada de las Lavaderas. Mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5162116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Valdelinares&lt;/a&gt; also provide exceptional panoramic views.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to explore while cycling?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can cycle past the historic Alcalá Castle, which offers unbeatable views, or visit the 18th-century Sanctuary of La Virgen de la Vega. The town itself boasts a charming old quarter with Moorish influence, and several hermitages like Loreto and San Roque dot the landscape, often providing beautiful vistas.

Q: Is parking available for road cyclists in Alcalá De La Selva?
A: Alcalá De La Selva, being a hub for outdoor activities, generally offers parking options within the town or near popular trailheads. It's advisable to check local signage for specific parking regulations and availability, especially during peak seasons.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation for cyclists?
A: Yes, Alcalá De La Selva and surrounding villages like Valdelinares offer various services for visitors. You can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options to refuel and rest after your rides. The town's old quarter is a good place to explore for local establishments.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Alcalá De La Selva?
A: The road cycling routes in Alcalá De La Selva are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 18 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the challenging climbs, the panoramic views across the Sierra de Gúdar, and the tranquility of the quiet minor roads through extensive forests.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging climbs in the Alcalá De La Selva area?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its demanding ascents. The climb to Valdelinares, which has been featured in the Vuelta a España, and sections of the Port of Gudar are notable for their steep gradients, providing a significant challenge for experienced road cyclists.
tips: The Sierra de Gúdar is a Spanish mountain range of the Iberian Mountain Range located in the province of Teruel. The municipality of Gúdar —which gives its name to the mountain range— is located in its foothills, very close to Valdelinares and its ski resort.
Port at 1828m altitude. The road on the north side is in pretty bad shape.
The port of Villarroya separates the Sierra de Gúdar. From the Villarroya viewpoint we will have magnificent views of the town of Villarroya de los Pinares.
On the north side, from Gudar, it has no difficulty. From Alcala de la Selva it is a long incline but without steep slopes. One of the most accessible in the area.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty and terrain like for this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult and demands a high level of fitness. You should expect mostly firm, paved surfaces, though some sections may feature rougher macadam or even unpaved segments, requiring good bike handling skills. The mountainous nature of the Sierra de Gúdar means consistent ascents and descents.

faqs.question: What are the key highlights or notable features along the route?
faqs.answer: The route takes you through the scenic Sierra de Gúdar mountain range, known for its high peaks and diverse landscapes. You'll encounter several mountain passes, including Sierra de Gúdar, Puerto de Valdelinares, and Gudar Pass. Keep an eye out for the Col sign! 😁, a popular spot for cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Gúdar region can experience snow on its summits during winter, so the best time to cycle this route is generally from late spring through early autumn. This avoids the coldest temperatures and potential snow, offering more favorable conditions for road cycling.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle in the Sierra de Gúdar area?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or access the general Sierra de Gúdar area. You are free to enjoy the natural beauty of the region.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car near the starting point in Alcalá de la Selva?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Alcalá de la Selva is a common starting point for routes in the region. Look for public parking options within the town, which typically offers convenient access to the trail's beginning.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and significant elevation gain, this route is not recommended for families with young children or as a casual outing. It's also primarily a road cycling route, so it's not designed for dogs. For dog-friendly activities, you might consider other trails in the region.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with sections of other paths. You'll find it crosses parts of the Senda del Maestrazgo, [CIMA TE01] Peñarrota - Estación de Valdelinares * Virgen de la Vega, and [CIMA TE01] Peñarrota - Estación de Valdelinares * Linares de Mora.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll be cycling through the stunning Sierra de Gúdar, a mountain range known for its high peaks and diverse landscapes within the Iberian System. Expect picturesque views of rugged mountains, often with summits over 2,000 meters, and an authentic connection with the Spanish countryside.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or rest stops along the route?
faqs.answer: The route traverses a mountainous and relatively rural area. While specific cafes or rest stops are not highlighted directly on the trail, it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks. Alcalá de la Selva, your starting and ending point, will have amenities for refreshments.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a challenging road cycling trip like this?
faqs.answer: For a difficult road cycling route of this length and elevation, you should bring appropriate cycling gear, plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit, a first-aid kit, and navigation tools (like a GPS device or the komoot app). Layered clothing is also recommended due to potential temperature changes at higher elevations.
